attributes.description: Alerts when async-invoked Lambda functions show high AsyncEventAge, indicating events are aging in the internal queue.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] High async event age

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Async Event Age

### Triage Steps
1. AsyncEventAge applies to asynchronously invoked functions (S3, SNS, EventBridge, etc.).
2. Check for sustained errors causing retries that backlog the queue.
3. Review throttling — rejected async invokes queue and retry.
4. Compare `AsyncEventsReceived` traffic with successful `Invocations`.
5. Inspect DLQ and destination configuration for failed event routing.

### Escalation
Rising async event age is a silent backlog signal — events may eventually expire or flood retries.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND MetricName == "AsyncEventAge"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_async_age_ms = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/AsyncEventAge`)
  BY FunctionName, cloud.region
// Async queue age threshold (ms): events waiting too long to be processed
| WHERE max_async_age_ms > 60000
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(FunctionName, "/", cloud.region)
| SORT max_async_age_ms DESC
| LIMIT 10
